mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities through a lower discount rate and weaker cash-yield competition, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is declining.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation rate, real rates turn more negative and equity multiples expand; this supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not show positive median 20-day SPX returns, or if the inflation-minus-policy spread is not associated with a positive conditional hit rate above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which should pressure risk assets and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher UNRATE levels fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the 2-year rolling Rank IC is not consistently below -0.03 in periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative and UNRATE rises simultaneously, the market receives a synchronized recession warning that should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is negative and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration in payrolls and unemployment does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 40% for the bearish sign.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS does not correspond to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses sign consistency across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST after a prior housing boom signals fading interest-rate-sensitive demand and weaker cyclical growth, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% from its 6-month high
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts drawdowns do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns or if the drawdown signal has no edge relative to unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO improves while CPIAUCSL growth slows, the economy gets a favorable supply-side impulse: real output strengthens without renewed inflation pressure, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is falling
failure_condition: Reject if the joint output-up/inflation-down condition does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the result equally well.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock rebound, rising INDPRO and PAYEMS together indicate broad real-activity normalization, which should support a short-term equity bounce and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous gains in industrial production and payrolls fail to improve 5-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the effect disappears once either series is removed.
